political.s: Planet Fitness maintains a bipartisan political stance, with minimal direct corporate donations ($28,318 in 2024) while spending $206,000 on lobbying efforts. The company pursues business-friendly legislation and has historically been subject to individual executive donations, some of which generated controversy when disclosed.

charity.s: Planet Fitness has contributed over $12 million to Boys & Girls Clubs of America since 2016 through its Judgement Free Generation initiative, focusing on youth wellbeing, kindness programs, and fitness access. The company provides approximately $1+ million annually and conducts volunteer activities across hundreds of organizations.
charity.focus: Youth wellbeing, anti-bullying, fitness access, Boys & Girls Clubs of America

environment.s: Planet Fitness tracks and reports emissions data with focus on energy efficiency, having switched to LED lighting company-wide in 2015 and implementing solar panels and energy-saving systems. The company became the first fitness brand to achieve WELL Health-Safety rating and reports Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions, though lacks specific reduction targets.

labor.s: Employee reviews on Glassdoor (3.3/5 stars) cite low wages (often barely above minimum), limited part-time benefits, heavy workloads, and variable management quality. An OSHA violation case from 2024 raised safety compliance concerns. However, the company prohibits retaliation for protected labor activities and maintains a code of ethics.

dei.s: Planet Fitness hired CEO Colleen Keating in 2024 who is a vocal DEI proponent and signed the CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion pledge. The company maintains a 3.8/5 DEI rating from employees and emphasizes its 'Judgement Free Zone' philosophy centered on inclusivity, though this has generated political controversy regarding locker room policies.

animals.s: Not applicable - Planet Fitness is a fitness and wellness service provider with no connection to animal testing or animal-derived products.

guns.s: Not applicable - Planet Fitness operates fitness centers and does not manufacture, sell, or distribute firearms or related products.

privacy.s: Planet Fitness collects personal information including biometric data and sells or shares data with third parties for marketing and advertising purposes. Common Sense Media rated the service with a 'Warning' due to data profiling and third-party data sales. The company offers opt-out mechanisms but data collection exceeds strict service requirements.

execPay.s: CEO Colleen Keating earned $11.39 million total compensation in 2025 (95.4% in bonuses/stock), with a base salary of $1 million plus substantial equity awards. Average executive compensation was $218,743 annually. The CEO pay ratio and gap between executive and entry-level wages (~$10/hour) indicates significant disparity.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
